excluded_defs.name: deprecate_option
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand existing approach; existing free function uses validator mechanism, but new method takes a cleaner member-based approach

exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing deprecate_option() free function already shows the pattern (validator + description update). The task wants an Option::deprecated() method with the same effect but using a member variable approach so the formatter can check it directly.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Uses validator mechanism rather than a direct member, which would make it harder for the formatter to check the deprecated state cleanly

exploration_log.hindsight: None — the member-based approach was the right choice from the start.

key_decisions.reasoning: Cleaner for formatter checks; allows the formatter to display [DEPRECATED] without hunting through validators

solve_notes: Added `deprecated_message_` member to Option, a `deprecated(string)` setter, and `get_deprecated()` / `get_deprecated_message()` getters. In `_parse_arg()`, after the option is identified, emit a warning to stderr when `get_deprecated()` is true. In `make_option_opts()`, append `[DEPRECATED]` to the help text for deprecated options.
task_text:
Add an `Option::deprecated(std::string message)` method that marks an
option as deprecated. When a deprecated option is used, the parser
should emit a warning to stderr (or a configurable callback) with the
deprecation message, while still accepting the value. Display
`[DEPRECATED]` in help text. Implement in `Option.hpp` and integrate
with `App::_parse_single()`.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: deprecate_option
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: existing free function shows the pattern used before; helps agent decide between validator-based vs member-based approach

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers all edited code. The thrash_preventing set adds the existing deprecate_option() free function — without seeing this, an AI agent might try to replicate its validator-based approach instead of the cleaner member-based approach, requiring backtracking.
